calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christina Nalchajian, via ZOOM, for Delvin Curry, via ZOOM, Administrator.

The court points counsel to the issue of the absence of timesheets with the request for allowance of extraordinary fees. Counsel provides oral advocacy in support of her request including, bit not limited to, the number of hours spent on this matter, the several trips to the office, adding that this has been far from a normal probate. The court and counsel discuss the history of this matter specifically regarding when the Salinas Law Group had represented her client. The court points to the 13101 affidavits used by some of the successors to the successors in interest. The court advises that it intends to make an exception to allow Michael Mim's share of the estate to be distributed despite the use of the 13101 affidavits by some of the successors of the successors in interest.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of A Second Status Report Or A Petition For Final Distribution
Off Calendar Aug-28-2019 Continued To Sep-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christina Nalchajian for Personal Representative.

The Court stated that it is looking for a petition for final distribution. Ms. Nalchajian stated that the property has finally been sold, that they had two or three weeks of wrangling with Chase Bank regarding the blocked account, requested four months and stated that they are working on the accounting. Ms. Nalchajian also stated that the proceeds from the sale are in two blocked accounts at Chase and Wells Fargo. Status on close of estate is continued to September 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court advised Counsel that if she is not prepared to file a petition for final distribution by then, she will need to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Removal Of Administrator Charlotte Mims
Off Calendar Jul-01-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Reuben Sundeen for petitioner Delvin Curry.

In response to the court's inquiry, Mr. Sundeen confirmed that he has still had no cooperation or response from the co-administrator. The Court granted the petition for removal of administrator Charlotte Mims as co-administrator, and the petition for letters of administration for Delvin Curry as sole administrator (subject to review of the orders).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy John Boncato. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-09-2016 Continued To Jun-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Continued To May-09-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruben Sundeen for and with (petitioner) Delvin Curry. Bruce Feder, Guardian ad Litem for (beneficiary) James Mims.

The Court stated that it needs a petition to remove Ms. Mims. Mr Sundeen stated that he is happy to file the petition, that the appointment was never effected and asked about filing a motion to reconsider the order. The Court cited 8480(c)and stated that this is what needs to be done. Mr. Feder asked if an order to suspend could be issued so that they could move forward, that suspending Ms. Mims powers could allow Mr. Curry's petition to go forward and that Ms. Mims has had several weeks to move forward. Mr. Sundeen stated that he is happy to submit an ex parte petition for special administration, that they agree that they need to move forward, that they have been unable to get bond waivers and that maybe a suspension is appropriate. The Court stated that it is choosing the initial course suggested. Mr. Feder stated that it is possible that filing a petition to remove may encourage Ms. Mims to move forward and asked for a short continuance to see if she does file a resignation. The petition for letters of administration was continued to May 9, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
